description: Overall experience is very promising and very much satisfied. I will recommend to all of my friends and companies.
author: Sanjib S.
pros: Powerful updates management tool. Easy to use and deploy software and applications. Easy for device management.
cons: I struggled sometime like while deploying a file to users computer. I was trying to deploy a file to users computer which i struggled a lot

review_title: Something all IT Admins need to remove one more headache from your role
description: Very good. Their support is responsive and their product is good value for money. I've used other patching and auditing systems in the past and they do one thing only. Automox does all of the essentials and more.
author: Adrian C.
pros: Easy to use, reliable and trustworthy. You setup your scheduled tasks and you can trust Automox to deliver results with minimal interference.
cons: Lack of user-installed software scanning. (Software where the uninstall goes into the users registry, not HKLM)

review_title: Almost but not quite there yet
author: Jimmy Y.
pros: Easy deployment and patching, able to do integrations
cons: not a good rmm tool and not quite there yet with its reporting functions

description: It is a very reliable tool that saves our team a lot of time by keeping all our remote computers safe and updated automatically.
author: Verified Reviewer
pros: It automates updates for Windows, Mac, and Linux very well. It saves us a lot of manual work every week.
cons: The search and reporting pages are sometimes slow to load. It can be hard to find specific data quickly.
author_duration: Used the software for: Less than 6 months

review_title: Powerful and Flexible Patching - What more can IT want?
description: Overall, my experience has been fantastic with Automox. The tool has saved me and my team so much time, and has closed many gaps when it comes to security and compliance. And their support is top notch - they have effectively and efficiently answered any question or issue (we haven't had many issues) when we need it.
author: Zac T.
pros: Automox strikes a great balance between power and usability. The two standout features for me are Worklets and Patch Schedules. Worklets make it incredibly easy to automate complex configurations and remediations across platforms, while Patch Schedules allow precise control over when and how updates are applied—critical for minimizing business disruption. The UI is intuitive, and deployments are typically reliable and fast, even in remote environments.
cons: This is going to be very nitpicky, as it's hard to say that I DISLIKE any component. My main concern is with some of the reporting metrics—specifically around CVE exposure and time-to-remediate (TTR). For example, a critical vulnerability might show as “80 days exposed” in the dashboard, but that figure reflects the public discovery date, not when it was actually detected on the device. This can make it harder to evaluate internal performance and compliance. I’d also like to see more clarity or filters in vulnerability timelines. Lastly, while not entirely Automox’s fault, getting Apple M-Series devices into a patch-ready state takes extra manual steps, which adds to deployment friction.

review_title: Keep your network upto date with Automox
description: Automox is a very good product when it comes to patch Management, application installation and it help to keep the IT environment very safe and upbto date.
author: Anuradha B.
pros: Cloud base product Easy to use console Administration is much more easier UI/UX is very attractive Many 3rd party applicatons also can patch
cons: Bandwidth consuption is little bit high when patch updating. In Asian countries like sri lanka, the bandwidth cost is very high.

description: I did a proof-of-concept product evaluation of Automox in November of 2023. We we're looking to replace a competitors product called "Patch My PC". Hands down it was a superior Patch Management product replacement.
author: Anthony C.
pros: Automox allows you to patch various operating systems using one application, making updates easier across different environments. It greatly cuts down the time needed for patch management, reducing the time spent on each machine from 15-30 minutes to just a few minutes, which saves many hours each month. The ability to create custom tools and the strong reporting features are very useful, supporting daily IT management and compliance during audits. Automox has significantly lowered the number of man-hours required for patching, potentially saving hundreds of hours. Managing updates and exclusions of patches on specific machines, like servers that need a certain version of Java, is straightforward, improving operational efficiency.
cons: Seriously nothing comes to mind. It's just works like its marketed as such. I would recommend this product to anyone looking for an effective Patch Management tool.

description: Support and Service has been excellent. The account management team has also been very engaging to ensure any quesitons or issues are addressed.
author: Miguel M.
pros: Simplicity and the lightness of the agent
cons: Lack of expanded reporting. You will most likely have to leverage API to get extract the data you are looking.

description: it has a been a amazing tool to deal with Threat management process . just loved it & would recommend to everyone
author: Chetna R.
pros: the available worklets to work with . they are super friendly
cons: the group customization . its very difficult to manage

description: Our experience has been great. Very simple to use and understand. Support is top notch and quick to respond. Makes vulnerability patching and risk assessment attainable to even tech level employees.
author: Martin R.
pros: Easy to use interface and dashboard. I love being able to be away from the office and know that anyone on my team can spend just a few minutes to figure out all the data and info needed to complete tasks.
cons: I think the worklet catalog could be larger. Also the remote desktop feature could be more robust with features to make it a true selling point.

description: It is making the IT admin life easier by providing patch update status and automated patch update distribution and bandwidth management for patch update distribution.
author: Uththara K.
pros: Automox is an easy-to-use patch management solution that will automate the patch update distribution among the organization networks.
cons: Price of the Automox is not affordable for many companies in developing countries.
author_duration: Used the software for: Less than 6 months

review_title: Automox for System updates and PowerShell Scrips just works
description: I've been using Automox for over 3 years, and for the most part, they have been a lifesaver. We use it to perform weekly software and Microsoft Windows updates, to push out new or updated versions of software, and to occasionaly run PowerShell scripts on machines. It works great when we use it like this, and Automox provides some great training (which I highly reccommend taking) to help you figure out the platform, give you some shortcuts, and assist with the reasons the platform behaves the way it does.
author: Dan C.
pros: Super easy to get initial set up completed. If you need additional ways to update or install software, or to make changes to a system, there are a ton of prewritten worklets to get you going.
cons: Automox doesn't work natively in a 64 bit environment, so you need to call the WOW6432Node to make scripts work.

review_title: Automox: A Powerful Cloud-Based Patch Management Solution with Room for Improvement
description: Overall, Automox is a solid cloud-based patch management solution that simplifies endpoint security and compliance. It’s particularly useful for organizations looking to automate OS and third-party application updates across Windows, macOS, and Linux devices without needing on-premise infrastructure.
author: B Karthik K.
pros: Automox has several strong points, especially when it comes to automated patch management and endpoint security. Here are some of the key pros: 1) Cloud-Based Management 2) Cross-Platform Support 3) Automated Patching 4) Custom Policies & Scripting and ETC
cons: While Automox is a strong patch management solution, there are some limitations and areas for improvement: 1) Limited Third-Party Patch Catalog 2) Pricing 3) Agent Dependency 4) Limited Native Reporting & Customization

review_title: Great product and worth the look.
description: We use it for general patching and critical updates. We use the remote assist function to replace team viewer.
author: Jason P.
pros: Easy to use and setup. Great tool for patching and remote assist.
cons: Remote assist feature works but does have drawbacks. Depending on the internet connection the destination device is on.

description: Overall experience has been good. The tool has helped optimizing the patching processes.
author: Adesh W.
pros: I like the feature of worklet catalog which helps in creating policies to configure devices.
cons: Processes get stuck in Queue if the device was not online for few days and to clear the status we either have to reinstall the agent service or contact support.

description: We can patch the organization's Windows devices and third-party applications.
author: Lahiru S.
pros: They support patch development for Windows and third-party solutions
cons: They only provide a SaaS solution and do not offer an on-premises option with Automox.

description: I would recommend this to everyone. Very easy to use, billing is easy too and dashboard is neat.
author: Hani S.
pros: Very easy to deploy on Macs with installer package. Very useful dashboard. Makes it easy to see patch levels.
cons: Need updated code to accomodate M1 Mac and such without the use of worklets.

description: Its been great. It has helped us as a company yo keep our devices up to date.
author: Verified Reviewer
pros: I like the granularity that comes with Automox. I can make policies for most of the things that I can think of.
cons: With apple silicon macbooks there seems to be a lot of compatibility errors.

description: Amazing, best option you can get on tje market
author: Fletcher P.
pros: Great easy to use, easy menus, easy UI, good service
cons: Nothing really, it has a nice dashboard and is great

description: It does the job, excellent software for patching updates, restarting machines remotely etc
author: Nicola D.
pros: patching function, remote reboot option.
cons: sometimes it takes too long to register a device

description: excellent, it does what it's suppose to do
author: Charles G.
pros: Easy of use, and the worklet catalog is amazing
cons: Cost, per device and you need to have a strong understanding of power shell.

description: My overall experience with Automox is great there support is great to work with and very knowledgeable.
author: Kyle S.

review_title: a very useful tool for managing large scale enterprise
description: very good overall, minus the points I made in the cons section

cons: the UI can be a bit confusing, also the client swapper being hidden away at the top is a bit of an inconvenience.
author_duration: Used the software for: Less than 6 months

description: Overall positive, some minor issues but it fills its use case for us
author: Sam W.

review_title: Patch and Software Management in one
description: Good experience, easy to use, easy to deploy, can find what I need on their forums
author: Verified Reviewer
pros: The flexibility to write your own tasks and create custom policies
cons: It could use better reporting, right now you generate a PDF which just shows the basics
